summary: Security analysis completed with 2 high, 1 medium severity findings discovered across 3 total findings.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find download functions
 * @description Finds functions containing 'download' or 'fetch' in their name in relevant files
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity recommendation
 * @id py/find-download-functions
 * @tags functions
 *       naming
 */

import python

from Function f
where 
  (f.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%download%") or f.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%fetch%"))
  and f.getLocation().getFile().getBaseName() in ["package_index.py", "easy_install.py"]
select f, "Function potentially related to downloading: " + f.getName()
findings.hypothesis: Functions handling downloads may be vulnerable to command injection if they pass URLs to shell commands.

message.text: Function potentially related to downloading: download
Function potentially related to downloading: fetch_distribution
Function potentially related to downloading: fetch

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Command execution in VCS download functions
 * @description Finds system command executions within specific download functions in setuptools/package_index.py
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id py/vcs-command-execution
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 */

import python
import semmle.python.dataflow.new.DataFlow
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

from Function f, DataFlow::CallCfgNode call
where
  f.getName() in ["_download_git", "_download_svn", "_download_hg"]
  and f.getLocation().getFile().getBaseName() = "package_index.py"
  and call.getScope() = f
  and (
    call = API::moduleImport("subprocess").getMember(["Popen", "call", "check_call", "check_output", "run"]).getACall()
    or
    call = API::moduleImport("os").getMember(["system", "popen"]).getACall()
  )
select call, "System command execution in " + f.getName()
findings.hypothesis: VCS download functions execute system commands, potentially using unsanitized URLs.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Command Injection in VCS download functions
 * @description Traces flow from function parameters to system command execution in VCS download helpers.
 * @kind path-problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id py/vcs-command-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 *       external/cwe/cwe-088
 */

import python
import semmle.python.dataflow.new.DataFlow
import semmle.python.dataflow.new.TaintTracking
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

module VcsCommandInjectionConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    exists(Function f, DataFlow::ParameterNode p |
      f.getName() in ["_download_git", "_download_hg", "_download_svn"]
      and p.getScope() = f
      and source = p
    )
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(DataFlow::CallCfgNode call |
      (
        call = API::moduleImport("os").getMember("system").getACall()
        or
        call = API::moduleImport("subprocess").getMember(["call", "check_call", "Popen", "run"]).getACall()
      )
      and sink = call.getArg(0)
    )
  }
}

module VcsCommandInjectionFlow = TaintTracking::Global<VcsCommandInjectionConfig>;
import VcsCommandInjectionFlow::PathGraph

from VcsCommandInjectionFlow::PathNode source, VcsCommandInjectionFlow::PathNode sink
where VcsCommandInjectionFlow::flowPath(source, sink)
select sink.getNode(), source, sink, "Potential command injection from $@.", source.getNode(), "parameter"
findings.hypothesis: User-controlled URLs passed to VCS download functions are used in system commands without sanitization.
